# Mend

> Mend is a free, open-source (MIT) accessibility auditor for Chrome. It scans
> the active tab against WCAG 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 using the axe-core rule engine,
> entirely on your device and with no network requests, and reports each issue
> with the fix first and the explanation underneath. An optional, separate cloud
> dashboard can store audit history and generate VPAT-format conformance
> reports; the extension itself works fully offline and never requires an
> account. Like every automated tool, Mend finds a subset of accessibility
> problems — it cannot make a site accessible or certify it as compliant, and
> the documentation here says so wherever conformance comes up.

## Docs

- [Docs index](https://mend-a11y.com/docs): Plain-language guides to
  accessibility conformance and the law.
- [VPATs and ACRs](https://mend-a11y.com/docs/vpats-and-acrs): What a VPAT
  (the blank template ITI publishes, currently 2.5Rev) and an ACR (the
  completed report) are, the four template editions, the conformance
  vocabulary — Supports, Partially Supports, Does Not Support, Not
  Applicable, Not Evaluated — who asks for one and why, how to read one
  critically, and how to produce a credible one. States plainly that
  automated testing covers only a subset of each success criterion and
  cannot by itself support a conformance claim.
- [Accessibility laws and legal compliance](https://mend-a11y.com/docs/accessibility-laws):
  Which laws reference web accessibility and what each points at — ADA
  Title II (WCAG 2.1 AA; DOJ compliance dates extended in April 2026 to
  26 April 2027 and 26 April 2028), ADA Title III (no codified web
  standard; genuinely unsettled), Section 508 (WCAG 2.0 A and AA), the
  European Accessibility Act (applies since 28 June 2025) with EN 301 549
  (harmonised at v3.2.1, based on WCAG 2.1), the EU Web Accessibility
  Directive, the UK's Equality Act 2010 and PSBAR 2018 (WCAG 2.2 AA), plus
  brief notes on Canada and Australia. **General educational information,
  not legal advice**; every date was verified against primary sources on
  the review date shown on the page, and this material dates quickly.

## Product

- [Home](https://mend-a11y.com/): What Mend does, and how its issue reports are
  written.
- [Pricing](https://mend-a11y.com/pricing): The extension is free forever. Plans
  cover the optional cloud dashboard only.
- [Privacy](https://mend-a11y.com/privacy): On-device processing, what the
  optional dashboard stores, and the third parties involved.
- [Support](https://mend-a11y.com/support): FAQ, GitHub Issues, and contact.
